nodes.name: zterm_ui
nodes.responsibility: High-level UI abstraction layer re-exporting zterm_ui_core with platform-specific implementations

nodes.name: fonts
nodes.responsibility: Font management, glyph rendering, and font fallback logic

nodes.name: rendering
nodes.responsibility: GPU-accelerated rendering using wgpu and glyph atlas caching

nodes.name: windowing
nodes.responsibility: Window lifecycle, event handling, and display management

nodes.name: GlyphCache
nodes.responsibility: Efficient caching of rendered glyphs to reduce GPU overhead

nodes.name: atlas
nodes.responsibility: Texture atlas management for batched glyph rendering

nodes.name: wgpu
nodes.responsibility: wgpu backend for cross-platform GPU rendering

nodes.name: zterm_ui_core
nodes.responsibility: Core UI abstractions re-exported by zterm_ui

hyperedges.name: GPU Rendering Pipeline
hyperedges.responsibility: Efficient GPU-accelerated text and UI rendering with font fallback

hyperedges.name: Platform Abstraction Layer
hyperedges.responsibility: Hide OS-specific window and font handling behind unified API

design_patterns.rationale: zterm_ui re-exports zterm_ui_core and adds platform-specific modules without duplicating core types
GlyphCache + TextureAtlas reduce redundant GPU uploads and draw calls
wgpu backend → atlas → glyph_cache → fonts provides layered GPU abstraction

architectural_rationale.wgpu_choice: Cross-platform GPU abstraction supporting Windows/macOS/Linux with Vulkan/Metal/DX12/WebGPU
architectural_rationale.glyph_caching: Terminal UI renders high-frequency glyph updates; caching dramatically reduces GPU pressure
architectural_rationale.atlas_batching: Texture atlases combine thousands of glyphs into single draw calls for performance
